rationale: The US-Iran peace deal announcement and Hormuz reopening continue from last cycle — no new qualitative change for Poland; energy price relief persists as background; other headlines have minimal Polish relevance.

top_events.title: US, Iran confirm ceasefire; peace deal to be signed Friday
top_events.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening confirmed last cycle continues — a formal signing is now announced for Friday, a minor incremental step. Energy price relief for Poland persists but no new qualitative shift.

top_events.title: Stock markets soar, oil falls as US-Iran deal confirmed
top_events.summary: Falling oil prices benefit Poland's energy costs and inflation outlook indirectly, but this is a continuation of the trend established last cycle — no new qualitative leap.

top_events.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
top_events.summary: Anti-G7 protests in Geneva are a distant event with no direct impact on Polish daily life; minor background noise for EU residents.
